pr.title: feat: bootstrap MemMapFS — WAL, zero-copy block storage, Tantivy search, async API
pr.body:
Implements the full MemMapFS project from scratch: a database-less, local-first memory and state sync file system for AI Agents, backed by an append-only WAL, memmap2 zero-copy block reads, and an embedded Tantivy full-text index.

## Storage (`storage.rs`)
- `LogManager` — length-prefixed bincode WAL; `replay()` reconstructs state linearly from offset 0, tolerating truncated tail records from unclean shutdowns
- `BlockStorage` — sequential appends to partitioned 64 MiB chunk files; reads via `memmap2::Mmap` for zero kernel-copy access

## Engine (`engine.rs`)
- `MemMapEngine` — `BTreeMap<u64, MemoryMetadata>` index + `HashMap` KV store
- `AgentState` broadcast via `tokio::sync::watch`; `send_replace` keeps the channel live with no active subscribers

## Search (`search.rs`)
- `SearchProvider` wraps a Tantivy `MmapDirectory` index
- Commits after each `index_memory` call for immediate searchability; reader uses `OnCommitWithDelay` reload policy

## Public API (`lib.rs`)
All mutable state behind `Arc<RwLock<…>>` — `MemMapFS` is `Clone`able and safe to share across tasks.

```rust
let fs = MemMapFS::init(".memmap_fs_root").await?;

let id = fs.append_memory("context payload", vec!["tag".into()]).await?;
let hits = fs.query_memory("context").await?;

fs.update_state(AgentState { status: "running".into(), last_updated_id: id, .. Default::default() }).await?;
```

WAL replay is exercised across two independent sessions in the integration test suite, verifying crash recovery semantics.
pr.author: Copilot
